source: IPIP 50-item Big-Five Factor Markers (Goldberg, 1992; public domain, https://ipip.ori.org/new_ipip-50-item-scale.htm). We use the canonical 50-item IPIP scale (10 items per domain) rather than BFI-44 because all IPIP items are in the public domain and freely redistributable; BFI-44 items (John & Srivastava, 1999) require attribution and copyright notice. File name retains 'bfi44' for skill compatibility.
scale: 1=Very inaccurate, 2=Moderately inaccurate, 3=Neither accurate nor inaccurate, 4=Moderately accurate, 5=Very accurate
response_format: Respond 1-5. For reverse-keyed items (key='-'), score is reversed as (6 - raw) before summing domain.

items.text: I am the life of the party.
I talk to a lot of different people at parties.
I don't like to draw attention to myself.
I don't mind being the center of attention.
I feel little concern for others.
I am not interested in other people's problems.
I am not really interested in others.
I take time out for others.
I make people feel at ease.
I make a mess of things.
I get chores done right away.
I often forget to put things back in their proper place.
I am exacting in my work.
I am relaxed most of the time.
I change my mood a lot.
I have difficulty understanding abstract ideas.
I am not interested in abstract ideas.
I do not have a good imagination.
I am quick to understand things.
I spend time reflecting on things.

scoring.instructions: For each domain, reverse reverse-keyed items (6 - raw) and average the 10 items. Resulting domain score is on 1-5 scale.

extraversion.source: Johnson (2014), 'Measuring thirty facets of the Five Factor Model with a 120-item public domain inventory: Development of the IPIP-NEO-120', Journal of Research in Personality 51: 78-89. Adult US sample N=619,150. Domain converted to 1-5 metric.
extraversion.note: Reported raw mean ~36.5/50 on IPIP-50; converted to per-item 3.65 in some reports. Goldberg's IPIP-50 norm table (ipip.ori.org) reports ~3.25 for extraversion in community samples.

agreeableness.source: Goldberg IPIP-50 norm table, ipip.ori.org; cross-checked with Johnson (2014) IPIP-NEO-120 adult US norms.

conscientiousness.source: Goldberg IPIP-50 norm table, ipip.ori.org; cross-checked with Johnson (2014) IPIP-NEO-120.

neuroticism.source: Goldberg IPIP-50 norm table, ipip.ori.org; cross-checked with Johnson (2014) IPIP-NEO-120. Note: IPIP labels this 'Emotional Stability' reverse-scored; reported here as Neuroticism (higher = more neurotic).

openness.source: Goldberg IPIP-50 norm table, ipip.ori.org (IPIP calls this 'Intellect/Imagination'); cross-checked with Johnson (2014) IPIP-NEO-120.
